name: Michaelis-Menten enzyme kinetics
conventions: [S] is the substrate concentration (mol/L or M)
V_max is the maximum reaction rate, attained as [S] -> infinity
K_M is the Michaelis constant: substrate concentration at which v = V_max / 2 (units of M)
applies to a single enzyme + single substrate, irreversible product formation
assumes quasi-steady-state for the enzyme-substrate complex (d[ES]/dt approx 0)
does not apply to allosteric enzymes (use Hill equation) or multi-substrate kinetics
expectedLimits: [S] = K_M:  v = V_max / 2  (operational definition of K_M)
competitive inhibitor: K_M' = K_M (1 + [I]/K_I), V_max unchanged
non-competitive inhibitor: V_max' = V_max / (1 + [I]/K_I), K_M unchanged
references: Michaelis & Menten (1913) — Die Kinetik der Invertinwirkung
